title: Elbrus MCST Platform Stack
body:
# Elbrus MCST Platform Stack

**Platform stack:** `pc_elbrus` · **Era:** 2001–2026 · **Vendor:** MCST · **Count:** 8

E2K VLIW architecture with **Elbrus-8CB (2018)** eight-core server die, DDR4 MC, PCIe switch, and x86 binary translation layer.

## Stack doctrine

This page lists every combinatorics leaf stamped `platform_stack: pc_elbrus` plus companion dies emitted from `field-cpu-companion-seed.json`. Host and guest CPUs matching the stack's `match` rules receive `always_with` linkage at combinatorics build — no manual wiring.

## Manufacturing notes — companion dies

- **Elbrus E2K System Hub** (MCST, 64-bit) — E2K on-chip ring + memory hub — VLIW host
- **Elbrus DDR4 MC** (MCST, 64-bit) — 4-channel DDR4 memory controller — Elbrus-8CB
- **Elbrus PCIe Switch** (MCST, 64-bit) — PCIe gen3 switch — server expansion
- **Elbrus I/O Hub** (MCST, 64-bit) — SATA USB Ethernet south — MCST platform
- **Elbrus x86 Translator** (MCST, 64-bit) — Binary translation layer — x86 guest on VLIW host

## Catalog ids

The `chip_ids` array holds all **8** entries for this platform stack, sorted alphabetically. Run `field-ironclad-chips-combinatorics.py combinatronic` for path percents and imprint renders.

*Stack: pc_elbrus · seed: data/field-cpu-companion-seed.json · citation: ironclad:chips:3*
